article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

reasoning: Classification is likely one step off; the op-ed has a mild conservative slant despite a both-sides frame. Confidence is slightly high for a centrist call. Evidence cites authorial framing rather than quotes, but indicators are somewhat vague. Also, the model output used markdown fences, violating the required JSON-only format.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: Article reads Lean Right due to author’s critical framing of liberal views on policing and emphasis on legitimacy of law enforcement, while still critiquing conservatives; model labeled Center (adjacent).
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.75 is on the high end for a mixed/both-sides piece where subtle right-lean cues exist; slightly overconfident.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: Indicators generally reference author’s own narration and structure, but are broad and not tied to specific quoted phrases from the author; limited textual specificity.
